# SplitLaunch

> Package-first A/B testing for customer-owned pages that AI agents can install, configure, run, and report on.

SplitLaunch is an A/B testing platform for teams that keep their own website.
It provides the browser package, pixel, external command API, agent skills,
redirect and assignment logic, conversion tracking, and reporting API.

## Key URLs

- Website: https://www.splitlaunch.dev
- Live agent skills: https://www.splitlaunch.dev/api/skills
- API skills endpoint: https://www.splitlaunch.dev/api/external/v1/skills
- API command endpoint: https://www.splitlaunch.dev/api/external/v1/commands
- Install docs: https://www.splitlaunch.dev/docs/install
- API docs: https://www.splitlaunch.dev/docs/api

## Important Docs

- Agent operating manual: /api/skills
- API skills file: /api/external/v1/skills
- Install guide: /docs/install
- API command guide: /docs/api
- NPM package: @splitlaunch/ab

## Command Envelope

{
  "command": "experiments.create",
  "input": {}
}

POST https://www.splitlaunch.dev/api/external/v1/commands
Authorization: Bearer sl_live_...
Content-Type: application/json

## What Agents Can Do

- Create and select projects, each with its own public pixel ID.
- Add allowed customer-owned domains before launching redirecting tests.
- Install @splitlaunch/ab and retrieve the public pixel ID through the CLI.
- Create, update, list, launch, pause, and end URL-based A/B experiments.
- Configure control URLs, variation URLs, traffic allocation, targeting,
  URL pattern rules, and redirect parameter preservation.
- Create and list conversion goals fired by the browser pixel.
- Pull experiment details, charts, acquisition channels, and usage summaries.
- Open the billing portal for subscription management.

## Agent Safety Rules

- Never put a private API key in browser code.
- Browser code only receives the public pixel ID, for example px_abc123.
- Use the private API key only in terminal, server, CI, or agent-side calls.
- Prefer creating experiments in draft, then verify URLs, domains, and goals
  before setting the experiment to running.
- Use projectId when the account has multiple projects.
